ground_truth.description: Photo from ground level looking up at facade of Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore in Firenze. Iconic architecture but angle might make it tricky.
ground_truth.photographer_description: Photo taken from ground level looking up at the facade of Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore in Firenze. Giotto's Campanile (bell tower) is barely visible. It should be able to recognize the iconic architecture, but the angle might (but should not) make it tricky. Daytime, clear weather.

details.description: Low-angle view of the richly decorated west façade of the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (Florence Cathedral/Il Duomo). The façade is clad in polychrome marble—white, green, and pink—with dense Gothic-Revival ornament: pointed arches, gables, arcades of niches filled with statues, elaborate sculptural portals, and large circular rose windows. Golden-ground mosaics are visible in the lunettes above the main portals. Warm late-afternoon light strikes the stone under a clear sky. A section of dark metal scaffolding is erected on the right side for restoration. At the extreme upper-right edge, a corner of Giotto’s Campanile with matching marble patterning is visible.

details.description: The image shows the ornate polychrome marble façade of the Florence Cathedral (Basilica di Santa Maria del Fiore) viewed from below, likely in the Piazza del Duomo. The façade features alternating green, pink, and white marble panels, a series of arched niches housing statues, and intricate decorative bands. A large circular rose window dominates the center above the main portal, flanked by smaller windows and sculptural elements. The central entrance is crowned by a triangular pediment with relief sculpture. Scaffolding is visible on the right side of the façade, indicating restoration work. The sky is clear and blue, with warm light highlighting the textures and colors of the stonework.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical shot capturing the ornate western facade of the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (the Duomo) in Florence, Italy. The facade, designed in the Gothic Revival style, features elaborate geometric patterns of polychrome marble in white, green, and pink. The composition is dominated by the central section, which includes a large rose window with intricate tracery, rows of statues in Gothic niches, and a central portal topped with a triangular wimperg containing a mosaic of Christ Enthroned with the Virgin Mary and St. John the Baptist. To the right of the central portal, black metal scaffolding is erected, obscuring part of the architecture. In the upper right corner, a segment of Giotto's Campanile (bell tower) is visible, displaying matching marble patterns. The sky is a clear, pale blue.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical shot of the ornate west facade of the Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore (Florence Cathedral). The facade is constructed from white, green, and pink marble arranged in complex geometric patterns. It features a large central rose window, numerous statues of saints and biblical figures in niches, and a grand central portal with a golden mosaic in its lunette. To the right, the lower portion of Giotto's Campanile is visible, mirroring the cathedral's polychrome marble style. Black metal scaffolding is erected on the right side of the main entrance, suggesting restoration work. The lighting is soft and warm, likely from the late afternoon sun, casting a glow on the lower architectural details against a clear, pale blue sky.

details.description: Upward view of the richly ornamented west facade of Florence’s cathedral at golden hour. The facade shows polychrome marble in green, white, and pink, pointed gables, deep archivolts, niches filled with statues of saints, and a large central rose window. A mosaic above the main portal is visible, and temporary scaffolding stands on the right side. A small portion of the striped bell tower appears at the extreme upper right against a clear sky.

details.description: Low-angle close-up of the ornate façade of the Florence Cathedral (Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore) in Piazza del Duomo, Florence. The front is clad in polychrome marble panels (green, pink, white) with intricate Gothic Revival detailing, a large circular rose window, three portal arches, and numerous niches with statues. Scaffolding is visible on the lower right of the façade. A clear blue sky forms the backdrop; the composition emphasizes the cathedral's monumental, decorative exterior characteristic of Florence's medieval-Gothic architecture.

details.description: Upward-angled view of the richly ornamented west facade of Florence Cathedral (Duomo di Firenze), showing its polychrome marble cladding in white, green, and pink, pointed Gothic gables, rose windows, arcades of sculptural niches with saints, and a mosaic tympanum over a portal. Warm late-day light highlights the carvings. A section of scaffolding is present near one portal. At the extreme edge, part of Giotto’s Campanile (the cathedral’s bell tower) is visible, confirming the Piazza del Duomo setting.

details.description: An elaborately decorated Gothic cathedral façade in polychrome marble (green, white, pink) with numerous statues in niches, decorative arches, and a large circular rose window. The façade features a triangular pediment with relief sculptures and a painted religious scene in a curved niche on the right; scaffolding is visible along the façade's right side, suggesting restoration. The architecture is Italian Gothic, with ornate detailing and a layered, portal-centered composition typical of Umbria or Tuscany cathedrals (likely Orvieto Cathedral). The image captures the building from a ground-level angle against a pale blue sky.

details.description: A steep, low-angle photograph capturing the ornate facade of the Florence Cathedral (Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore) and a portion of Giotto's Campanile against a clear blue sky. The image highlights the cathedral's iconic polychrome marble exterior in shades of white (Carrara), green (Prato), and pink (Maremma), arranged in intricate geometric patterns. The primary subject is the upper section of the 19th-century Gothic Revival facade, featuring twisted columns, statues in niches, and a large, ornate gable. A mosaic lunette above the central portal is clearly visible, depicting Christ enthroned with the Virgin Mary and St. John the Baptist. To the top left, the cornice and upper details of Giotto's Bell Tower are visible. Scaffolding is present on the right side of the facade, indicating maintenance work.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical shot of the ornate Neo-Gothic facade of the Florence Cathedral (Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore). The facade is decorated with intricate geometric patterns of white, green, and pink marble. Key features include a large central rose window, multiple tiers of statues in niches depicting various religious figures, and a golden mosaic above a side portal. Scaffolding is visible on the right side of the facade, indicating ongoing restoration work. In the upper left corner, a section of the similarly styled Giotto's Campanile is visible against a clear, pale blue sky.

details.description: Upward-angled close view of the richly ornamented west facade of Florence Cathedral (Duomo di Firenze), clad in polychrome marble panels of white, green, and pink/red. The facade shows layered pointed gables, deep archivolts filled with sculpture, niches with statues, and two elaborate rose windows. A gold-ground mosaic crowns one pointed portal, and temporary black scaffolding covers part of the central entrance. At the edge of the frame a tall, square bell tower with matching striped marble bands and arcaded windows (Giotto’s Campanile) is visible. Warm late-afternoon light strikes the intricate Gothic and neo-Gothic detailing against a clear blue sky.

entities.name: Florence Cathedral (Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore; Duomo di Firenze)
entities.notes: Principal cathedral of Florence. West facade completed 1871–1887 under Emilio De Fabris in neo-Gothic style; clad in white Carrara, green Prato, and red Siena marbles; features rose windows, sculptural niches, and mosaic tympana.

entities.name: Giotto’s Campanile
entities.notes: Bell tower of Florence Cathedral. Designed by Giotto (begun 1334), continued by Andrea Pisano and completed by Francesco Talenti (1334–1359). Polychrome marble revetment harmonizing with the cathedral.

details.description: An ornate exterior facade of a grand European cathedral is shown from a ground-level, oblique angle against a clear blue sky. The polychrome marble surface displays intricate sculptural details, arched arcade windows, and a row of niches containing statues. A prominent circular rose window sits within a richly decorated central section, surrounded by decorative moldings and triangular pediments. Part of the right-hand façade is obscured by scaffolding, and a curved apse or portal features painted figures. The overall aesthetic suggests Italian Gothic/Neo-Gothic architectural styling with white, green, and pink marble detailing.
